channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Fly tipping beds and other items, 20th July
item.description:
Fly tipping large items

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Staddon Close, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tip of tyres and rubbish, 20th July
item.description:
There is a recurring issue with rubbish dumping/fly-tipping at this location. Currently, tyres and multiple bags of rubbish have been left lying here for two weeks. Because this is an ongoing problem near the entrance of our homes, it creates a serious eyesore, hazards, and encourages further dumping. Can we fix this.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kingston Crescent, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish on the pavement, 20th July
item.description:
These houses constantly leave rubbish on the pavement. I have the full address so please contact me directly. I don’t know how long the rubbish has been there but it’s not the first time.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Whittington Road, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping, 20th July
item.description:

item.title: 1-123 Redbridge Garden, 20th July
item.description:
NTUP officer found the main entrance intercome door is unscured please fix it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Redbridge Gardens, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Builder’s rubble, 20th July
item.description:
Hi this guy just turned up on Saturday night and was unloading buildings bricks and wood, I ask him not to put it there and he continued to unload from a wheelbarrow and didn’t give a shit. He had the cheek to have sunglasses on with his hod up. When are the council going to sort this out a put a camera up to stop this it’s out of hand.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Strathnairn Street, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Selsea Place, Hackney, London

item.title: The edges around a manhole cover are breaking up, 20th July
item.description:
Hole around man hole cover is big enough for a child’s foot to fit in. Hole is getting bigger

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wolfe Crescent, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near my bin, 20th July
item.description:
Dog mess again in the garden

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6-8, Toussaint Walk, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside the Pharmacy, 20th July
item.description:
What's "wrong" is that Brent placed lots of food waste bins along the length of Salusbury Road. These are constantly filthy, they stink, they are used as a dumping point for other rubbish, they leak, they take up valuable pavement space, and are a magnet for flies (and probably rats, too). They are a environmental health hazard and should be removed ASAP. Failing that, the bin outside the pharmacy is bursting with stinking garbage and litter and needs to be dealt with! A total waste of tax payers money and all to save a few bags of food waste!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salusbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
